12:43:19	 From Tom : Be careful with your spam complaints. We tested changing the from line and it did give a good boost in orders/revenue (I remember it being close to 100%). However, our spam complains went up by like 400% since people didn't recognize who the email was from

12:44:55	 From Maranda : Would you recommend a certain number of days for testing before you kill an ad that shows low conversions and start another?

12:45:25	 From Tom : Good point

12:48:58	 From Matthew Katz : We saw that Trump Health Bombshell subject line earlier...can we take a quick look at the lift that pointed to?

12:50:07	 From Stefan Georgi : The one I just linked is mine which beat that control and is the main one running now

12:50:19	 From Matthew Katz : Got it. Thanks.

12:51:18	 From Kate Aseltine : All caps in the subject line… [FDA ALERT] 3 WAYS TO IDENTIFY FAKE CBD

12:51:32	 From Kate Aseltine : Do you test ALL CAPS versus no caps?

12:55:27	 From Tanner Henkel : we have tested caps vs. no caps a decent amount to our list. The winners depend on the offer and context… so if it’s something like the above example “WARNING” “ALERT” etc the caps wins out… but if it’s for a gut health offer putting “GUT HEALTH” in the subject line vs. just “gut health” doesn’t seem to give significant lift.
